owasp_top10: A09:2021 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
mitre_attack: T1213.003 - Data from Information Repositories: Code Repositories
cwe: CWE-532 - Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
CWE-312 - Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information

threat.title: API Key Exposure via Application Logs in Express.js and AWS Lambda
threat.description: API keys, secrets, and authentication tokens inadvertently logged to application logs, CloudWatch Logs, or third-party logging services enable attackers to extract credentials and gain unauthorized access to cloud resources, databases, and third-party services. This is one of the most prevalent security issues in serverless and containerized applications, particularly in AWS environments where verbose logging is common during development and often persists into production.

attack.scenario:
1. Developer enables verbose logging in Express.js application with debug-level configuration that logs request headers and bodies
2. Application deployed to AWS Lambda logs API requests containing Authorization headers with bearer tokens or API keys to CloudWatch Logs
3. Attacker gains read access to CloudWatch Logs through compromised IAM credentials, insider threat, or misconfigured log group permissions (logs:GetLogEvents)
4. Attacker extracts API keys from log entries using pattern matching (e.g., grep for 'Authorization: Bearer', 'x-api-key:', 'apikey=')
5. Attacker validates extracted credentials against target services (AWS APIs, third-party services like Stripe, SendGrid, OpenAI)
6. Using valid credentials, attacker escalates privileges, exfiltrates sensitive data, or launches supply chain attacks by accessing integrated services
prerequisites: Application logs request headers, query parameters, or request bodies containing credentials
CloudWatch Logs or centralized logging system retains logs for extended periods (30+ days)
Attacker has read access to log streams via IAM permissions, log aggregation service, or compromised credentials
No log sanitization or redaction mechanisms in place
API keys are long-lived and not rotated regularly

cve_references.description: jQuery prototype pollution vulnerability exploited to access AWS credentials logged in application error messages
Netty HTTP/2 implementation logged sensitive headers including API keys in debug mode, affecting AWS Lambda functions using Netty-based frameworks
Lodash prototype pollution leading to exposure of environment variables containing AWS credentials in error logs

real_world_breaches.impact: 2.15 million customers affected; 10 years of vehicle location data exposed due to AWS access key in public GitHub repository
real_world_breaches.root_cause: AWS access keys hardcoded in source code and committed to public GitHub repository, subsequently logged in application error traces

real_world_breaches.impact: Over 30,000 organizations potentially affected; customer secrets including AWS credentials, API keys, and tokens compromised
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Malware on employee laptop exfiltrated session tokens; investigation revealed extensive logging of secrets in debug mode across customer pipelines

bug_bounty_reports.summary: AWS Lambda function logs contained Stripe API keys in CloudWatch, allowing unauthorized payment processing and customer data access
Express.js application logged full OAuth2 tokens in Morgan access logs, enabling account takeover for 50,000+ users

security_research.title: The State of Secrets Sprawl 2024
security_research.key_finding: Over 12.8 million secrets detected in public GitHub repositories in 2023, with 75% being API keys and cloud credentials. CloudWatch Logs identified as a major exposure vector in AWS environments.

security_research.title: AWS Lambda Security Best Practices
security_research.key_finding: CloudWatch Logs should never contain credentials. Implement log sanitization and use AWS Secrets Manager for credential management in Lambda functions.

mitigations.title: Implement Log Sanitization Middleware for Express.js 4.x
mitigations.description: Create custom Express.js middleware to automatically redact sensitive data from request/response objects before logging. This prevents API keys, tokens, and credentials from being written to logs in the first place.
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated
validation_steps: Send test request with Authorization header containing fake API key
Verify logs show '***REDACTED***' instead of actual key value
Check CloudWatch Logs for any leaked credentials using regex search
Test error logging to ensure stack traces don't expose secrets
Validate JSON logs can still be parsed after redaction

mitigations.title: AWS Lambda Environment Variable Encryption and Secrets Manager Integration
mitigations.description: Use AWS Secrets Manager to store API keys and credentials instead of Lambda environment variables. Retrieve secrets at runtime using AWS SDK v3 with proper IAM permissions and implement local caching to minimize API calls.

tools.name: AWS Secrets Manager
tools.cost: $0.40 per secret per month + $0.05 per 10,000 API calls

tools.name: AWS KMS
tools.cost: $1.00 per key per month + $0.03 per 10,000 requests

nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are issued, managed, revoked
validation_steps: Create secret in AWS Secrets Manager with test API key
Configure Lambda IAM role with secretsmanager:GetSecretValue permission
Deploy Lambda function and test secret retrieval
Verify CloudWatch Logs contain no secret values (search for 'sk-', 'Bearer', etc.)
Test secret rotation without Lambda function redeployment
Monitor AWS CloudWatch Metrics for SecretsManager API call counts

mitigations.title: AWS CloudWatch Logs Data Protection and Pattern Filtering
mitigations.description: Implement CloudWatch Logs data protection policies to automatically detect and mask sensitive data patterns in log streams. Use subscription filters to alert on potential credential exposures and set appropriate retention policies.
nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are aggregated and correlated
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
RS.AN-1 - Notifications from detection systems are investigated
validation_steps: Deploy CloudWatch Logs data protection policy to test log group
Send test log entry containing fake API key (e.g., 'sk-test123456789')
Verify CloudWatch Logs Insights shows redacted value: ***REDACTED***
Confirm subscription filter triggers Lambda alert function
Check SNS topic receives security alert notification
Review CloudWatch Metrics dashboard for APIKeyExposureEvents metric
Test alarm triggers when threshold exceeded

indicators: Unusual API calls from unexpected geographic locations using valid credentials
Spike in CloudWatch Logs GetLogEvents API calls from non-monitoring IAM principals
API key usage from multiple IP addresses simultaneously
High-value API operations (e.g., S3 PutBucketPolicy, IAM CreateAccessKey) from recently created or inactive accounts
Third-party service alerts (Stripe, SendGrid) indicating API key abuse or rate limit violations
CloudWatch Logs queries containing patterns like 'Authorization', 'x-api-key', 'bearer' from unusual IAM users

log_queries.query:
# Detect API keys in Lambda logs
fields @timestamp, @message, @logStream
| filter @message like /(?i)(api[_-]?key|authorization|bearer|x-api-key)\s*[:=]\s*['\"][a-zA-Z0-9\-_\.]{20,}['\"]/ 
| filter @message not like /REDACTED/
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 100

# Detect AWS credentials in logs
fields @timestamp, @message, @logStream
| filter @message like /AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}/  # AWS Access Key pattern
| filter @message not like /example|sample|test|placeholder/
| stats count() as exposureCount by @logStream
| sort exposureCount desc

index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudwatch:logs 
| rex field=_raw "(?<potential_key>(sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}|AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}|eyJ[a-zA-Z0-9\-_]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9\-_]+\.[a-zA-Z0-9\-_]+))" 
| search potential_key=* 
| stats count by log_group, log_stream, potential_key 
| where count > 0 
| eval severity="critical" 
| table _time, log_group, log_stream, potential_key, severity

sequence by host.name
  [file where file.path : "/var/log/app/*.log" and 
   process.command_line : ("*Authorization: Bearer*", "*x-api-key:*", "*apikey=*")]
  [network where destination.port in (443, 80) and 
   source.ip != "10.0.0.0/8" and 
   event.action == "connection_attempted"]
| within 5m

// Detect API key exposure in Azure Application Insights logs
AppTraces
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where Message contains "api" and (Message contains "key" or Message contains "bearer" or Message contains "authorization")
| where Message !contains "REDACTED"
| extend PotentialKey = extract(@"([a-zA-Z0-9\-_\.]{30,})", 1, Message)
| where isnotempty(PotentialKey)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated) by AppRoleName, Message, PotentialKey
| where ExposureCount > 3
| order by ExposureCount desc

tools.name: AWS CloudWatch Logs Insights
tools.usage: Use queries above to detect patterns in CloudWatch Logs across all Lambda functions and log groups
